ERP Consultants and Implementation Specialists require SAP, Oracle, business process modeling, project management, and change management. AI and ML Engineers require Python, TensorFlow, Keras, AI algorithms, NLP, and data preprocessing. Business Intelligence Analysts require SQL, Tableau, Power BI, data mining, business analytics, and decision support systems. Blockchain Developers require Ethereum, Hyperledger, smart contracts, cryptography, and distributed ledgers. FinTech Professionals require FinTech platforms, blockchain, digital payments, regulatory compliance, and data analytics.

The following data is from the Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs) table: Graduates will excel as ethical and responsible cybersecurity professionals in globally competitive environments. Graduates will contribute to society through innovation and research in cybersecurity and allied domains. Graduates will demonstrate lifelong learning through continuous professional development. The following data is from the Programme Specific Outcomes (PSOs) table: Graduates will be able to analyze, design, and implement secure computing systems. Graduates will be capable of applying security principles and practices to solve real-world problems in cybersecurity. Graduates will possess the skills to investigate and respond to cyber threats and vulnerabilities. The following data is from the Programme Outcomes (POs) table: Graduates will be able to apply knowledge of mathematics, science, and engineering fundamentals to solve complex cybersecurity problems. Graduates will be able to identify, formulate, and analyze complex cybersecurity problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. Graduates will be able to design solutions for complex cybersecurity problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. Graduates will be able to use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions. Graduates will be able to create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex cybersecurity activities with an understanding of the limitations. Graduates will be able to apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice. Graduates will be able to understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. Graduates will be able to apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. Graduates will be able to function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings. Graduates will be able to communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions. Graduates will be able to demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments. Graduates will be able to recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

CONTENT: Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry)

No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry)

Intake / No. of Seats: Total — 30 (Government — 15, Management — 15)

Eligibility: 10+2 system of Education. Must have secured a pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in the qualifying examination.

Curriculum & Syllabus (2021 Regulation): <https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf>

Scope for Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.

#### Vision:

To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Communication Engineers through sustainable practices.

#### Mission:

M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in communication systems through innovative teaching and learning methodology.

M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society.

M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability.

Note: NRI Quota applies — 5% of sanctioned seats (approximately 1–2 seats) are reserved under NRI quota as per standard Anna University / TNEA norms.

All rooms are well furnished with modern amenities: cot, mattress with pillows, bed-spreads, individual cup-boards, chair-table with lamp and wall hangers. Each room accommodates only 2 persons. A hygienic canteen is available for dining within the premises. An entertainment hall with LCD-TV, indoor game facilities, reading room with newspapers and popular magazines are available. The Hostel has land-line telephone and Wi-Fi internet access. The College Main Library and the Computer Centre are kept open till 7:00 PM for the benefit of hostellers. Students will feel more at home and concentrate on their studies, facilitated by a serene atmosphere filled with greenery.

There are about 71 rooms, accommodating 3 girl students per room. All rooms are well furnished with: cot, mattress with pillows, bed-spreads, individual cup-boards, chair-table with lamp, and wall hangers. Each room is provided with bath and toilet facilities, wash basin and mirror.

An entertainment hall with LCD-TV, indoor game facilities, reading room with newspapers and popular magazines are available. The Hostel is connected with land-line telephone and Wi-Fi internet access. Library and Computer facility are made available till 9:00 PM.

chunks.text: The IQAC or Internal Quality Assurance Cell is a quality enhancement and sustenance system of MSAJCE. It is a facilitative and participative management system. The IQAC is a consistent driving force for planning, guiding and monitoring Quality Assurance and Quality Enhancement activities of the college. It was established on 20-08-2012 as per the NAAC guidelines. The IQAC assures the stakeholders i.e., students, parents, teachers, staff, management, funding agencies and society in general of the accountability and transparency in the quality education concern in the institution. It also coordinates various activities like application for accreditation, preparation of AQAR, organization of workshops and seminars on quality related themes, and promote quality circles.

It also coordinates various activities like application for accreditation, preparation of AQAR, organization of workshops and seminars on quality related themes, and promote quality circles. In pursuance of the National Action Plan of the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC), Bangalore, for performance evaluation, assessment and accreditation and quality upgradation of institutions of higher education, the NAAC proposes that every accredited institution should establish an Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) as a post-accreditation quality sustenance measure. Since quality enhancement is a continuous process, the IQAC will become a part of an institution's system and work towards realizing the goals of quality enhancement and sustenance. The prime task of the IQAC is to develop a system for conscious, consistent and catalytic improvement in the performance of institutions. The IQAC will make a significant and meaningful contribution in the post-accreditation phase of institutions. During the post-accreditation period, the IQAC will channelise the efforts and measures of an institution towards academic excellence. The guidelines provided will facilitate the institution in the creation and operation of the IQAC. The work of the IQAC is the first step towards the internalization and institutionalization of quality enhancement. Its success depends upon the sense of belongingness and participation it can inculcate in all the constituents of the institution. It will not be yet another hierarchical structure or record-keeping exercise in the institution; it will be a facilitative and participative voluntary system of the institution. The IQAC has the potential to become a vehicle for ushering in quality by working out intervention strategies to remove deficiencies and enhance quality. Quality circles in industries operate on similar lines. IQAC Page URL: <https://www.msjace-edu.in/iqac.php> NAAC Page: <https://www.msjace-edu.in/naac.php>

Hence devotion and commitment to improvement rather than mere institutional control is the basis for devising procedures and instruments for assuring quality. The right balance between the health and growth of an institution needs to be struck. The IQAC has to ensure that whatever is done in the institution for education is done efficiently and effectively with high standards. chunks.text: The following data is from the Programme Outcomes (PO) — B.Tech Information Technology, MSAJCE table: PO1 Engineering Knowledge means to apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 Problem Analysis means to identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. PO3 Design/Development Of Solutions means to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PO4 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems means to use research based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions. PO5 Modern Tool Usage means to create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations. PO6 The Engineer and Society means to apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice. PO7 Environment and Sustainability means to understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. PO8 Ethics means to apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. PO9 Individual and Team Work means to function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multi disciplinary settings. PO10 Communication means to communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large. PO11 Project Management and Finance means to demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work. PO12 Life long Learning means to recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

CONTENT: The following data is from the Programme Outcomes (PO) — B.Tech Information Technology, MSAJCE table: PO1 Engineering Knowledge means to apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 Problem Analysis means to identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. PO3 Design/Development Of Solutions means to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PO4 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems means to use research based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions. PO5 Modern Tool Usage means to create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations. PO6 The Engineer and Society means to apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice. PO7 Environment and Sustainability means to understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. PO8 Ethics means to apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. PO9 Individual and Team Work means to function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multi disciplinary settings. PO10 Communication means to communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large. PO11 Project Management and Finance means to demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work. PO12 Life long Learning means to recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

Job opportunities are available in various MNCs in service and product companies as System Engineers, Software Professionals, Network Engineers, System Analysts, System Administrators, Project Leaders, Education sector, System Architects and Academicians. The Information Technology (IT) industry in India is poised for strong growth over the next five years, driven by domestic and global trends. Businesses across all sectors are adopting Cloud computing, AI, Big Data, IoT, and Blockchain, so demand for cloud engineers, data scientists, AI specialists, and DevOps engineers will rise. The Cloud market in India is growing rapidly, and AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud will create high demand for cloud architects, cloud administrators, and cloud security specialists, and hybrid cloud solutions will generate opportunities in cloud migration, cloud operations, and cloud infrastructure management. AI is transforming healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and retail, and by 2030, demand will rise for data scientists, AI researchers, machine learning engineers, and AI-enabled software developers, requiring skills such as AI ethics, deep learning, and AI model deployment. With increasing cyberattacks and regulations (GDPR, Personal Data Protection Bill), demand for cybersecurity experts will grow, creating roles for ethical hackers, incident response professionals, security architects, and SOC analysts. India's cashless economy push requires blockchain developers, smart contract auditors, and DeFi specialists. The rollout of 5G will create demand for 5G network engineers, telecommunications architects, and telecom software developers, and IoT will see a boom in smart cities, agriculture, and healthcare. Government initiatives like Digital India, Smart Cities Mission, and Make in India will create roles for IT project managers, data engineers, IoT developers, and cloud solutions architects. India will continue as a global IT outsourcing hub with demand for application developers, IT consultants, systems integrators, and support specialists.

India will continue as a global IT outsourcing hub with demand for application developers, IT consultants, systems integrators, and support specialists. The following data is from the Employment Opportunities in IT Domain (2025–2030) section: Job roles in cloud computing and virtualization include Cloud Engineer, Cloud Solutions Architect, Cloud Security Specialist, DevOps Engineer, and Cloud Administrator. Required skills include AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Cloud security (IAM, encryption), Containers (Docker, Kubernetes), and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible). Relevant certifications are AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Microsoft Certified Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Google Cloud Professional Cloud Architect, and Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA). Job roles in artificial intelligence and machine learning include Data Scientist, AI Engineer, Machine Learning Engineer, NLP Specialist, and Deep Learning Engineer. Skills required are Python, R, Java; ML frameworks (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch); Data visualization (Matplotlib, Seaborn); and knowledge of AI/ML algorithms and statistical models. Certifications include Microsoft Certified Azure AI Engineer Associate, Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer, IBM AI Engineering Professional Certificate, and AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty. Cyber Security job roles include Security Analyst, Ethical Hacker (Penetration Tester), Security Architect, SOC Analyst, and Cybersecurity Consultant. Skills include risk management, ethical hacking tools (Metasploit, Burp Suite), firewalls, SIEM tools (Splunk, LogRhythm), and incident response and forensics. Certifications are CEH, CISSP, CISM, CompTIA Security+, and CISA. Blockchain and Fintech job roles include Blockchain Developer, Smart Contract Developer, Blockchain Architect, Cryptocurrency Expert, and Blockchain Security Engineer. Skills required are Blockchain platforms (Ethereum, Hyperledger, Solana), smart contract development (Solidity, Rust), and DApp development. Certifications include Certified Blockchain Developer, Ethereum Developer Certification, and Certified Blockchain Professional (CBCP). IT Infrastructure and Networking job roles include Network Administrator, IT Systems Engineer, IT Infrastructure Manager, Virtualization Engineer, and Network Security Engineer. Skills include TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP, VMware, Hyper-V, VPN, IDS/IPS, and firewalls. Certifications are CCNA, CompTIA Network+, CISA, and VMware Certified Professional (VCP). The following data is from the Expected Employment Growth Trends section: The Indian IT industry is expected to grow at an annual rate of 7-10%, especially in Cloud computing, Cyber security, AI, and data science.

chunks.text: The National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) is a methodology adopted by the Ministry of Education (MoE), Government of India, to rank institutions of higher education across the country. MSAJCE participates in NIRF rankings annually and submits data across three categories: Engineering, Overall, and Innovation. chunks.text: NIRF PDFs are hosted at base URL: https://www.msjce-edu.in/uploads/nift/ with naming convention: MSAJCE-ENGG-YYYY.pdf, MSAJCE-OVERALL-YYYY.pdf, and MSAJCE-INNOV-YYYY.pdf. A total of 10 PDF documents are available across 4 years, from 2023 to 2026. The following data is from the Document / Page table: The NAAC Page is available at https://www.msjce-edu.in/naac.php, the IQAC Page is available at https://www.msjce-edu.in/iqac.php, and the NIRF Page is available at https://www.msjce-edu.in/nirf.php. The ARIIA Certificate 2021 is located at https://www.msjce-edu.in/uploads/ariia/ARIIA-2021-CERTIFICATE.pdf, the Anna University Approval is located at https://www.msjce-edu.in/uploads/AnnaUnivApproval.pdf, and the AICTE Approval is located at https://www.msjce-edu.in/uploads/AICTE-APPROVAL.pdf. The Code of Conduct can be found at https://www.msjce-edu.in/uploads/naac/cdeofconduct.pdf, Best Practices are documented at https://www.msjce-edu.in/uploads/naac/bestpractices.pdf, and the Mandatory Disclosure is available at http://msajce-edu.in/uploads/MandatoryDisclosure.pdf. The Institutional Distinctiveness is detailed at http://msajce-edu.in/uploads/naac/7.3/7.3LH.pdf, the Annual Report AY2023-2024 is at https://www.msjce-edu.in/uploads/aqar/AnnualReport2023-2024.pdf, and Online Admission information is at https://enrollonline.co.in/Registration/Apply/MSAJCE. The following data is from the table with no headers: Fee Payment information is available at https://www.feepayr.com/, Exam Results can be found at https://results.msjce-edu.in/, AICTE Scholarship Schemes are detailed at https://www.aicte.gov.in/schemes/students-development-schemes, and the Grievance Cell information is at https://www.msjce-edu.in/grievanceredressalcommittee.php. The following data is from the MSJCE — General Contact Information table: The address is 34, Rajiv Gandhi Salai (OMR), Siruseri IT Park, Siruseri, Chennai – 603103. The phone number is +91 99400 04500, the email is msjce.office@gmail.com, and the website is www.msjce-edu.in. For grievances, contact grievance@msjce-edu.in, and for online admissions, visit https://enrollonline.co.in/Registration/Apply/MSAJCE. The NAAC information is available at https://www.msjce-edu.in/naac.php, the IQAC information is available at https://www.msjce-edu.in/iqac.php, and the NIRF information is available at https://www.msjce-edu.in/nirf.php.
